title: Bitter Uruguayan Mirin Empanada
description: A traditional Uruguayan dish with a twist, featuring a bitter and sweet glaze made with mirin. This hangover-cure empanada is a perfect treat any time of day.
instructions: In a saucepan, combine mirin, brown sugar, and 1 tablespoon of water over medium heat, stirring occasionally.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
In a separate pan, melt the lard over medium heat.
Add the eggs, cinnamon, ginger, and salt to the pan and scramble until the eggs are cooked through.
Add the onions to the pan and cook until they are translucent.
Add the butter to the pan and stir until melted.
Add the cooked egg mixture to the saucepan with the mirin glaze and stir to combine.
Butter a baking dish and place the empanada dough in the dish.
Pour the egg and mirin mixture into the empanada and top with cocoa nibs.
Bake the empanada for 25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Let the empanada cool for 5 minutes before serving.
